phase_1.audio: Audio environment is distracting with avg 63.9 dB exceeding the 60 dB acceptable threshold. All 4 confidence-filtered events labeled 'busy' with 3 Speech and 1 Ambient AI labels indicate a noisy, conversation-heavy environment unsuitable for focused study.
phase_1.vitals: Heart rate at 86.9 bpm is slightly elevated above the calm focus range (60-85 bpm), suggesting mild stress or activity. Steps averaging 36.25 per sample and distance of 32.99m per sample indicate consistent movement rather than seated study behavior.
phase_1.gps_motion: GPS shows drastic location instability with 331.5m displacement and 838.6m total travel, far exceeding stable study thresholds (<200m). Motion context confirms active behavior with 3 walking instances vs only 1 stationary, indicating the user was mobile throughout the session.

phase_2.correlations: All modalities converge on a pattern of active mobility rather than stationary study: noisy busy audio aligns with walking motion contexts, elevated heart rate correlates with continuous movement (36 steps/sample), and substantial GPS travel (838m) confirms the user was moving between locations. The Speech-heavy audio labels suggest social interaction or public spaces during transit.
phase_2.holistic_assessment: This 77-minute session shows clear evidence of active commuting or travel rather than concentrated study. The combination of distracting noise levels, consistent physical movement, elevated heart rate, and drastic location changes across all sensor modalities indicates minimal to no focused study activity occurred during this period.

phase_3.recommendations: 1) Establish a fixed, quiet study location to eliminate the 838m of travel and reduce ambient noise below 60 dB. 2) Schedule study sessions separate from commute/transit times when walking and movement are unavoidable. 3) Use noise-canceling headphones or find private spaces to avoid busy/speech-heavy environments that fragment attention.

reason: The session demonstrates very poor concentration conditions across all modalities. Audio at 63.9 dB with busy/speech labels exceeds distraction thresholds, heart rate of 86.9 bpm with 36 steps per sample indicates active movement rather than seated focus, and GPS travel of 838m with 3 walking contexts confirms the user was commuting or mobile throughout. This represents transit activity, not study concentration.
